company.what_they_do: Project Domesday is a B2B software/data intelligence platform focused on corporate ownership transparency and compliance screening for the defense contracting ecosystem. It ingests registries, filings, and curated sources to build and maintain a graph of corporate structure and beneficial ownership, propagating updates when control shifts through intermediaries. It tracks counterparty and supply chain exposure across programs — covering suppliers, investors, and adjacent firms — surfacing structural drift and downstream risk so primes, subcontractors, and partners can escalate early. It maps specific compliance regimes (CMMC, ITAR, Section 847, Section 889, and related) to reviewable evidence bundles and screening outputs aligned to how auditors and partners evaluate posture, retaining counsel where required. It also provides policy-bound review workflows — triage, investigation, and contract-adjacent — over its graph and provenance data, with audit logs, escalation paths, and configurable constraints.
raw_notes: Four product areas surfaced on homepage: (1) Ownership & Structure — registry/filing-fed ownership graph, beneficial ownership tracking, propagation of control shifts through intermediaries; (2) Counterparty & Supply — tiered risk tracking across programs for suppliers/investors/adjacent firms, structural drift and downstream exposure monitoring; (3) Compliance & Sanctions — CMMC, ITAR, Section 847, Section 889 and related regimes mapped to evidence bundles and screening outputs, counsel retained where required; (4) Review Workflows — analyst/operator triage, investigation, contract-adjacent workflows over 'Domesday's graph and provenance,' with audit logs, escalation paths, configurable constraints.

Language is clearly aimed at defense contracting market: references to 'primes, subcontractors, and partners,' 'eligibility,' 'contract-adjacent workflows,' and specific defense compliance regimes (CMMC, ITAR, Sec. 847/889).
Positioned as graph-native data intelligence, not a simple registry lookup — emphasis on provenance, propagation, and audit trail.

The product name 'Domesday' deliberately evokes the original 1086 Domesday Book — a comprehensive survey/record of ownership and control — as a brand metaphor for their ownership/compliance data graph.
Counsel retention language ('with counsel retained where required') suggests either in-house legal or legal-as-a-service component for compliance review.
No pricing, team page, about page, or blog visible from provided homepage content.
Site appears newly launched — no search engine indexing found.
Source: first-party homepage content provided directly from https://projectdomesday.com
